valid.name: exists-form

invalid.name: in-subquery
invalid.code:
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IN (SELECT id FROM other);
SELECT * FROM t WHERE x = ANY (SELECT id FROM other);

errors.message: Use `EXISTS (...)` instead of `IN (subquery)`. `IN` returns NULL when the subquery has any NULL row, which silently turns the row into a no-match; `EXISTS` is unambiguously boolean.
Use `EXISTS (...)` instead of `IN (subquery)`. `IN` returns NULL when the subquery has any NULL row, which silently turns the row into a no-match; `EXISTS` is unambiguously boolean.
